title: amphoricarpos autariatus subsp. autariatus
intro: Amphoricarpos autariatus subsp. autariatus, also known as the Korean angelica or Korean licorice root, is a subspecies of plant in the carrot family, characterized by its edible roots and leaves used in traditional medicine. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, which contribute to its various health benefits. The health benefits of Amphoricarpos autariatus subsp. autariatus include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory conditions, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as decoctions and infusions, are commonly used in traditional Korean medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
